mission.title: spire: Provide telemetry dashboard templates
mission.description: Provide telemetry dashboard templates. Requested by 13+ users.

steps.title: Check current spire deployment
steps.description:
Verify your spire version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n spire -l app.kubernetes.io/name=spire
helm list -n spire 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working spire installation.

steps.title: Review spire configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant spire configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n spire -l app.kubernetes.io/name=spire
kubectl get configmap -n spire -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=spire
```
In order to effectively operate SPIRE in a production environment, users need to develop visualization of SPIRE telemetry to have adequate observability into the system.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Provide telemetry dashboard templates
steps.description:
Add a JSON file to create a Grafana dashboard for visualizing Spire metrics provided by Prometheus.
Add Telemetry folder for metrics documentation.

- [X] Documentation updated?

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n spire -l app.kubernetes.io/name=spire
kubectl get events -n spire --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Provide telemetry dashboard templates" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Add a JSON file to create a Grafana dashboard for visualizing Spire metrics provided by Prometheus.
Add Telemetry folder for metrics documentation.

- [X] Documentation updated?

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with spire installed or the issue environment reproducible.
